task_text: Meridian Group has asked for a product demo of Helios in two weeks. Based on what you can find in the sandbox, write a short assessment (3-4 paragraphs) of whether we are ready to demo and what, if anything, needs to be resolved first. Save it as demo_readiness_assessment.txt.

Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Project Helios — QA Testing Log: Sprint 14
QA Engineer: Kenji Watanabe
Period: 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-11 (ongoing)

=== Test Suite: Authentication Flows ===
TC-401  OAuth token issuance              PASS
TC-402  Token refresh                     PASS
TC-403  Expired token rejection           PASS
TC-404  Invalid scope rejection           PASS
Suite result: 4/4 PASS

=== Test Suite: Data Pipeline — Batch Ingestion ===
TC-501  CSV ingestion (10k records)                  PASS
TC-502  JSON ingestion (10k records)                 PASS
TC-503  XML ingestion (5k records)                   PASS
TC-504  Date normalization (ISO 8601 input)          PASS
TC-505  Date normalization (MM/DD/YYYY input)        FAIL  [OPEN — HEL-D-014]
TC-506  Duplicate record detection                   PASS
TC-507  Large file ingestion (500k records)          FAIL  [OPEN — HEL-D-015]
Suite result: 5/7 PASS, 2 FAIL

=== Test Suite: Dashboard ===
TC-601  Data table rendering (10k records)           PASS
TC-602  Chart rendering — line chart                 IN PROGRESS
TC-603  Chart rendering — bar chart                  NOT RUN
TC-604  Load time benchmark (100k records)           NOT RUN
Suite result: 1/4 complete

=== Open Defects ===
HEL-D-014  Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input format.
           Affects ~40% of Meridian source data. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Blocked pending Meridian sample data (due 2026-07-15).

HEL-D-015  Large file ingestion (>200k records) causes memory overflow.
           Pipeline crashes at approximately 210k record mark. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Root cause under investigation.

Overall sprint 14 test status: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 FAIL (open), 3 not yet run.

Project Helios — Q2 Deliverables Checklist

[DONE]      Requirements documentation v1.0
[DONE]      System architecture design
[DONE]      API gateway implementation
[DONE]      Development environment setup
[DONE]      Data schema finalization
[IN-PROGRESS] Data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
[IN-PROGRESS] Client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
[OVERDUE]   Integration test suite setup (was due 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20)
[OVERDUE]   UAT environment provisioning (was due 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access)
[OVERDUE]   Performance benchmarking report (was due 2026-07-05; owner: Derek Okafor)

input.content:
Based on the current status of the Helios project, the platform is not yet fully ready for a product demo to Meridian Group in two weeks. While the API gateway is complete and the data ingestion module is 85% finished, the client dashboard—a critical component for any visual demo—is only 60% complete. Its v1 target delivery is slated for July 22, which leaves very little buffer before a demo. Additionally, integration testing is significantly lagging behind schedule at 40% complete.

Before we can confidently demo the product, we must resolve two high-severity defects identified in Sprint 14 QA testing. Most critically, there is a date normalization failure (HEL-D-014) regarding MM/DD/YYYY formats that affects approximately 40% of Meridian's source data. We are currently awaiting sample data from Meridian to fix this issue, and it is a strict blocker for demonstrating their specific data flows. Furthermore, a memory overflow defect (HEL-D-015) causes the pipeline to crash when ingesting files larger than 200,000 records, which could easily cause a catastrophic failure during a live demonstration.

There are also outstanding administrative and infrastructure dependencies to address. The license renewal for our third-party mapping library, GeoSync v2.1, is pending legal review and needs to be completed by July 14. Additionally, provisioning for the UAT environment is currently overdue because it is blocked by Meridian IT access. Securing this access is essential so we have a stable, staged environment to host the demo rather than relying on active development environments.

To be ready in two weeks, the team must prioritize finishing the core components of the client dashboard and resolving the date formatting defect with Meridian's data. If the large file ingestion crash cannot be fully patched in time, we will need to carefully scope the demo to use datasets well below the 200,000-record threshold. Successfully mitigating these issues will ensure a stable and professional showcase of the Helios platform.

content.text:
I have reviewed the sandbox files relevant to the Helios project and the Meridian Group. Based on the project status, testing logs, and deliverables checklist, I have drafted the readiness assessment. 

The 4-paragraph assessment has been written and saved as `demo_readiness_assessment.txt` in your working directory. It highlights the outstanding high-severity defects (notably a critical date formatting issue affecting Meridian's data), the incomplete client dashboard, and environmental blockers that must be resolved before the demo.
